summary: The correct time for first permanent molar calcification is approximately 20 weeks of gestation (~5 months), occurring after the initial formation of deciduous incisors (around 3–4 months). Therefore, stating they begin before deciduous incisors is inaccurate—calcification initiates later than decidual tooth bud emergence.
